videos.title: Irã alerta em caso de invasão terrestre pelo EUA
videos.description:
🚨 TENSÃO MUNDIAL EM ALTA!

Um comandante do exército do Irã fez uma declaração alarmante:
em caso de invasão terrestre, não haverá sobreviventes.

A ameaça foi direcionada principalmente aos Estados Unidos e seus aliados, aumentando ainda mais o risco de um conflito de grandes proporções no Oriente Médio.

🌍 O mundo acompanha com preocupação enquanto tropas se movimentam e o clima de guerra se intensifica.

⚠️ Estamos diante de uma simples ameaça… ou do início de algo muito maior?

#geopolítica #atualidades #noticias #mundo #políticainternacional #breakingnews #youtubeshorts
videos.transcript: Pesquisar transcrição Atenção, o mundo pode estar mais perto de uma guerra total e uma ameaça direta acabou de ser feita. Um comandante do exército do Irã deu uma ordem chocante. O major general Ratami do Exército do Irã ordenou que caso haja uma invasão terrestre inimiga, as forças iranianas devem agir sem deixar sobreviventes. Ou seja, uma resposta militar total e extremamente agressiva. Em termos simples, se tropas dos Estados Unidos e Israel entrarem por terra no Irã, a ordem seria eliminar completamente essas forças. Isso significa que qualquer tropa inimiga que entrar no país será completamente eliminada. O alerta é direcionado principalmente aos Estados Unidos e Israel, e o clima já é de tensão máxima no Oriente Médio. Movimentação de tropas, operações militares acontecendo e ameaças sendo trocadas diariamente. Se uma invasão acontecer, isso pode virar uma guerra em larga escala. segue para mais atualizações.

videos.description:
The year is 2025 and with bethesda teasing a return to the world of oblivion gamers are once again searching for a taste of skyrim. With the oblivion remaster looking to be a remake that fleshes out some skyrim systems like stealth archer builds I thought it would be a good idea to dive into a new games spin on the RPG formula. This game is Tainted Grail: The Fall of Avalon. It has split news outlets with half saying it is an indie skyrim and half saying its indie dark souls. For me the spiffing brit this game is perfectly balanced. Remember this game is NOT SKYRIM but perhaps just like skyrim we can find infinite money glitches, overpowered magic and an array of silly physics moments. 

If you are interested in the game you can grab it on steam sale here at some point or wait for its full 1.0 release: https://store.steampowered.com/app/14...

It is described as follows: Step into a dark reimagination of Arthurian legends in this first person, open world RPG. Explore a world stuck in everlasting autumn, witness falling legends and make meaningful choices woven into a complex, branching storyline. SO yeah it does indeed hit all of the lovely buzzwords that todd howard used to draw me into the world of tamriel and skyrim all those years ago. So lets hunt for bugs, glitches and exploits in this open world adventure.

videos.title: THIS IS NOT SKYRIM 2 - BECOMING A GOD In Tainted Grail A PERFECTLY BALANCED GAME
videos.description:
What Does It Take To Make A God In A Video Game.

The year is 2025 and with bethesda teasing a return to the world of oblivion gamers are once again searching for a taste of skyrim. With the oblivion remaster looking to be a remake that fleshes out some skyrim systems like stealth archer builds I thought it would be a good idea to dive into a new games spin on the RPG formula. This game is Tainted Grail: The Fall of Avalon. It has split news outlets with half saying it is an indie skyrim and half saying its indie dark souls. For me the spiffing brit this game is perfectly balanced. Remember this game is NOT SKYRIM but perhaps just like skyrim we can find infinite money glitches, overpowered magic and an array of silly physics moments. 

If you are interested in the game you can grab it on steam sale here at some point: https://store.steampowered.com/app/14...

It is described as follows: Step into a dark reimagination of Arthurian legends in this first person, open world RPG. Explore a world stuck in everlasting autumn, witness falling legends and make meaningful choices woven into a complex, branching storyline. SO yeah it does indeed hit all of the lovely buzzwords that todd howard used to draw me into the world of tamriel and skyrim all those years ago. So lets hunt for bugs, glitches and exploits in this open world adventure.

By that I mean it changes very few things and keeps in a lot of the jank we know and love. However the Developers and bethesda did have the time to patch out and remove some of This Elder Scrolls most famous exploits, bugs and glitches. Prepare to be amazed by the incredible combat skills and slow paced nightmare that is the exciting adventures in Starfield unarmed! So lets see if Starfield is a perfectly balanced game with no exploits or if perhaps todd has left in some bugs glitches and features for us to use. 

Starfield description: From the creators of Fallout 4 and Skyrim comes the next Bethesda AAA Game release in the form of the space rpg starfield! It was announced during Bethesda's E3 presentation in 2018. The game takes place in a space-themed setting, and is the first new intellectual property developed by Bethesda in 29 years and finally it is here! Today we will follow the adventure of reanu keeves a space adventurer on a quest to become the most overpowered character in starfield. We will see if its possible to bring back the skyrim stealth archer build in a brand new starfield build and see if we can stack modifiers, bugs and dupes to exploit the game so lets see what we can do!

videos.title: Knight Matthias GETS EXPOSED!! & Clearing Lioncrest Watchtower | Crimson Desert BLIND Part 4
videos.description:
In this episode of my Crimson Desert blind playthrough, I take on the Lioncrest Watchtower, solve a tricky puzzle, and claim the Visione Helmet—but the real challenge comes in an epic showdown with Knight Matthias. After the intense duel, I explore the city of Hernand, chatting with shopkeepers, meeting random townsfolk, and stocking up on much-needed gear for the next objective of clearing out the bandits from Springtide Mill.

⚔️ Main Highlights:

Clearing Lioncrest Watchtower

Solving the puzzle for the Visione Helmet

Boss fight: Knight Matthias

Exploring Hernand and shopping for supplies

💬 This series is a first-time blind playthrough—no guides, no spoilers! If you enjoy immersive storytelling, exploration, and combat, stick around and subscribe for more Crimson Desert episodes.

videos.title: Wooly the Sheep Was An IMPOSTER?!?! - Crimson Desert BLIND Part 5
videos.description:
Part 5 of my blind Crimson Desert playthrough hits like a bandit ambush at 3 AM: I save the mill, stumble on a field of dead cows, chase down a “sheep” that turns out NOT to be the kids’ sheep named Wooly (thanks, game), and clear out a creepy cave—only to discover the Goldleaves merchants are the real villains, hoarding sky-boulders for reasons that make about as much sense as a snowball in hell.

Dark humor, sarcasm, and zero spoilers until I accidentally stumble into them. If you like soulsborne vibes, weird animal betrayals, and merchant conspiracies, smash subscribe.

videos.title: Knight Matthias GETS EXPOSED!! & Clearing Lioncrest Watchtower | Crimson Desert BLIND Part 4
videos.description:
In this episode of my Crimson Desert blind playthrough, I take on the Lioncrest Watchtower, solve a tricky puzzle, and claim the Visione Helmet—but the real challenge comes in an epic showdown with Knight Matthias. After the intense duel, I explore the city of Hernand, chatting with shopkeepers, meeting random townsfolk, and stocking up on much-needed gear for the next objective of clearing out the bandits from Springtide Mill.

⚔️ Main Highlights:

Clearing Lioncrest Watchtower

Solving the puzzle for the Visione Helmet

Boss fight: Knight Matthias

Exploring Hernand and shopping for supplies

💬 This series is a first-time blind playthrough—no guides, no spoilers! If you enjoy immersive storytelling, exploration, and combat, stick around and subscribe for more Crimson Desert episodes.

videos.title: Wooly the Sheep Was An IMPOSTER?!?! - Crimson Desert BLIND Part 5
videos.description:
Part 5 of my blind Crimson Desert playthrough hits like a bandit ambush at 3 AM: I save the mill, stumble on a field of dead cows, chase down a “sheep” that turns out NOT to be the kids’ sheep named Wooly (thanks, game), and clear out a creepy cave—only to discover the Goldleaves merchants are the real villains, hoarding sky-boulders for reasons that make about as much sense as a snowball in hell.

Dark humor, sarcasm, and zero spoilers until I accidentally stumble into them. If you like soulsborne vibes, weird animal betrayals, and merchant conspiracies, smash subscribe.

videos.title: Knight Matthias GETS EXPOSED!! & Clearing Lioncrest Watchtower | Crimson Desert BLIND Part 4
videos.description:
In this episode of my Crimson Desert blind playthrough, I take on the Lioncrest Watchtower, solve a tricky puzzle, and claim the Visione Helmet—but the real challenge comes in an epic showdown with Knight Matthias. After the intense duel, I explore the city of Hernand, chatting with shopkeepers, meeting random townsfolk, and stocking up on much-needed gear for the next objective of clearing out the bandits from Springtide Mill.

⚔️ Main Highlights:

Clearing Lioncrest Watchtower

Solving the puzzle for the Visione Helmet

Boss fight: Knight Matthias

Exploring Hernand and shopping for supplies

💬 This series is a first-time blind playthrough—no guides, no spoilers! If you enjoy immersive storytelling, exploration, and combat, stick around and subscribe for more Crimson Desert episodes.

videos.title: Wooly the Sheep Was An IMPOSTER?!?! - Crimson Desert BLIND Part 5
videos.description:
Part 5 of my blind Crimson Desert playthrough hits like a bandit ambush at 3 AM: I save the mill, stumble on a field of dead cows, chase down a “sheep” that turns out NOT to be the kids’ sheep named Wooly (thanks, game), and clear out a creepy cave—only to discover the Goldleaves merchants are the real villains, hoarding sky-boulders for reasons that make about as much sense as a snowball in hell.

Dark humor, sarcasm, and zero spoilers until I accidentally stumble into them. If you like soulsborne vibes, weird animal betrayals, and merchant conspiracies, smash subscribe.

videos.title: Knight Matthias GETS EXPOSED!! & Clearing Lioncrest Watchtower | Crimson Desert BLIND Part 4
videos.description:
In this episode of my Crimson Desert blind playthrough, I take on the Lioncrest Watchtower, solve a tricky puzzle, and claim the Visione Helmet—but the real challenge comes in an epic showdown with Knight Matthias. After the intense duel, I explore the city of Hernand, chatting with shopkeepers, meeting random townsfolk, and stocking up on much-needed gear for the next objective of clearing out the bandits from Springtide Mill.

⚔️ Main Highlights:

Clearing Lioncrest Watchtower

Solving the puzzle for the Visione Helmet

Boss fight: Knight Matthias

Exploring Hernand and shopping for supplies

💬 This series is a first-time blind playthrough—no guides, no spoilers! If you enjoy immersive storytelling, exploration, and combat, stick around and subscribe for more Crimson Desert episodes.

videos.title: Wooly the Sheep Was An IMPOSTER?!?! - Crimson Desert BLIND Part 5
videos.description:
Part 5 of my blind Crimson Desert playthrough hits like a bandit ambush at 3 AM: I save the mill, stumble on a field of dead cows, chase down a “sheep” that turns out NOT to be the kids’ sheep named Wooly (thanks, game), and clear out a creepy cave—only to discover the Goldleaves merchants are the real villains, hoarding sky-boulders for reasons that make about as much sense as a snowball in hell.

Dark humor, sarcasm, and zero spoilers until I accidentally stumble into them. If you like soulsborne vibes, weird animal betrayals, and merchant conspiracies, smash subscribe.

videos.title: Knight Matthias GETS EXPOSED!! & Clearing Lioncrest Watchtower | Crimson Desert BLIND Part 4
videos.description:
In this episode of my Crimson Desert blind playthrough, I take on the Lioncrest Watchtower, solve a tricky puzzle, and claim the Visione Helmet—but the real challenge comes in an epic showdown with Knight Matthias. After the intense duel, I explore the city of Hernand, chatting with shopkeepers, meeting random townsfolk, and stocking up on much-needed gear for the next objective of clearing out the bandits from Springtide Mill.

⚔️ Main Highlights:

Clearing Lioncrest Watchtower

Solving the puzzle for the Visione Helmet

Boss fight: Knight Matthias

Exploring Hernand and shopping for supplies

💬 This series is a first-time blind playthrough—no guides, no spoilers! If you enjoy immersive storytelling, exploration, and combat, stick around and subscribe for more Crimson Desert episodes.

videos.title: Wooly the Sheep Was An IMPOSTER?!?! - Crimson Desert BLIND Part 5
videos.description:
Part 5 of my blind Crimson Desert playthrough hits like a bandit ambush at 3 AM: I save the mill, stumble on a field of dead cows, chase down a “sheep” that turns out NOT to be the kids’ sheep named Wooly (thanks, game), and clear out a creepy cave—only to discover the Goldleaves merchants are the real villains, hoarding sky-boulders for reasons that make about as much sense as a snowball in hell.

Dark humor, sarcasm, and zero spoilers until I accidentally stumble into them. If you like soulsborne vibes, weird animal betrayals, and merchant conspiracies, smash subscribe.

videos.title: Knight Matthias GETS EXPOSED!! & Clearing Lioncrest Watchtower | Crimson Desert BLIND Part 4
videos.description:
In this episode of my Crimson Desert blind playthrough, I take on the Lioncrest Watchtower, solve a tricky puzzle, and claim the Visione Helmet—but the real challenge comes in an epic showdown with Knight Matthias. After the intense duel, I explore the city of Hernand, chatting with shopkeepers, meeting random townsfolk, and stocking up on much-needed gear for the next objective of clearing out the bandits from Springtide Mill.

⚔️ Main Highlights:

Clearing Lioncrest Watchtower

Solving the puzzle for the Visione Helmet

Boss fight: Knight Matthias

Exploring Hernand and shopping for supplies

💬 This series is a first-time blind playthrough—no guides, no spoilers! If you enjoy immersive storytelling, exploration, and combat, stick around and subscribe for more Crimson Desert episodes.

videos.title: Wooly the Sheep Was An IMPOSTER?!?! - Crimson Desert BLIND Part 5
videos.description:
Part 5 of my blind Crimson Desert playthrough hits like a bandit ambush at 3 AM: I save the mill, stumble on a field of dead cows, chase down a “sheep” that turns out NOT to be the kids’ sheep named Wooly (thanks, game), and clear out a creepy cave—only to discover the Goldleaves merchants are the real villains, hoarding sky-boulders for reasons that make about as much sense as a snowball in hell.

Dark humor, sarcasm, and zero spoilers until I accidentally stumble into them. If you like soulsborne vibes, weird animal betrayals, and merchant conspiracies, smash subscribe.

videos.title: Knight Matthias GETS EXPOSED!! & Clearing Lioncrest Watchtower | Crimson Desert BLIND Part 4
videos.description:
In this episode of my Crimson Desert blind playthrough, I take on the Lioncrest Watchtower, solve a tricky puzzle, and claim the Visione Helmet—but the real challenge comes in an epic showdown with Knight Matthias. After the intense duel, I explore the city of Hernand, chatting with shopkeepers, meeting random townsfolk, and stocking up on much-needed gear for the next objective of clearing out the bandits from Springtide Mill.

⚔️ Main Highlights:

Clearing Lioncrest Watchtower

Solving the puzzle for the Visione Helmet

Boss fight: Knight Matthias

Exploring Hernand and shopping for supplies

💬 This series is a first-time blind playthrough—no guides, no spoilers! If you enjoy immersive storytelling, exploration, and combat, stick around and subscribe for more Crimson Desert episodes.

videos.title: Wooly the Sheep Was An IMPOSTER?!?! - Crimson Desert BLIND Part 5
videos.description:
Part 5 of my blind Crimson Desert playthrough hits like a bandit ambush at 3 AM: I save the mill, stumble on a field of dead cows, chase down a “sheep” that turns out NOT to be the kids’ sheep named Wooly (thanks, game), and clear out a creepy cave—only to discover the Goldleaves merchants are the real villains, hoarding sky-boulders for reasons that make about as much sense as a snowball in hell.

Dark humor, sarcasm, and zero spoilers until I accidentally stumble into them. If you like soulsborne vibes, weird animal betrayals, and merchant conspiracies, smash subscribe.

videos.title: Knight Matthias GETS EXPOSED!! & Clearing Lioncrest Watchtower | Crimson Desert BLIND Part 4
videos.description:
In this episode of my Crimson Desert blind playthrough, I take on the Lioncrest Watchtower, solve a tricky puzzle, and claim the Visione Helmet—but the real challenge comes in an epic showdown with Knight Matthias. After the intense duel, I explore the city of Hernand, chatting with shopkeepers, meeting random townsfolk, and stocking up on much-needed gear for the next objective of clearing out the bandits from Springtide Mill.

⚔️ Main Highlights:

Clearing Lioncrest Watchtower

Solving the puzzle for the Visione Helmet

Boss fight: Knight Matthias

Exploring Hernand and shopping for supplies

💬 This series is a first-time blind playthrough—no guides, no spoilers! If you enjoy immersive storytelling, exploration, and combat, stick around and subscribe for more Crimson Desert episodes.

videos.title: Wooly the Sheep Was An IMPOSTER?!?! - Crimson Desert BLIND Part 5
videos.description:
Part 5 of my blind Crimson Desert playthrough hits like a bandit ambush at 3 AM: I save the mill, stumble on a field of dead cows, chase down a “sheep” that turns out NOT to be the kids’ sheep named Wooly (thanks, game), and clear out a creepy cave—only to discover the Goldleaves merchants are the real villains, hoarding sky-boulders for reasons that make about as much sense as a snowball in hell.

Dark humor, sarcasm, and zero spoilers until I accidentally stumble into them. If you like soulsborne vibes, weird animal betrayals, and merchant conspiracies, smash subscribe.

videos.title: O Irã está em vantagem na guerra: a Armênia vai explodir, não se deixem arrastar para a estupidez...
videos.description:
Inscreva-se no canal do YouTube da 168.am e acompanhe as notícias, entrevistas e análises constantemente atualizadas da 168.am.

#Notícias #DaArmênia #NOTÍCIAS #168 #168am #Armênia #168TV

#ao vivo #notícias #168jam #Armênia #novostiArmenii #Novosti_Armenii_segodnya #Políticas_Armênias

#JeffreySachs #168TV #AraksMartirosyan #EntrevistaExclusiva #168am #Diplomata #Armênia #Irã #EUA #Israel #Geopolítica #PolíticaExterna #CáucasoDoSul #OrienteMédio #RelaçõesInternacionais #SegurançaRegional
#EUArmênia #RelaçõesRusso-Armênias #Trump2026 #GuerraHíbrida #DesenvolvimentoSustentável #TendênciasGlobais #EleiçõesParlamentares #AgendaDeSegurança
O convidado exclusivo de hoje no programa “Diplomat” da 168TV é o economista de renome mundial e autoridade líder em desenvolvimento sustentável, Jeffrey Sachs, Diretor do Instituto da Terra da Universidade de Columbia.

Em uma entrevista abrangente com o jornalista Araks Martirosyan, da 168.am, o Professor Sachs examina as principais tendências políticas globais, oferecendo uma avaliação completa do confronto entre os EUA e Israel com o Irã e delineando possíveis cenários finais, incluindo o papel dos mediadores internacionais. Ele também aborda aspectos-chave da agenda de política externa da Armênia, incluindo as relações Armênia-EUA, o “caminho de Trump”, a dinâmica armênio-russa e as perspectivas para a potencial adesão da Armênia à UE. Além disso, Sachs avalia os esforços do governo armênio para combater as ameaças híbridas nas próximas eleições parlamentares em cooperação com a UE, bem como as potenciais implicações dessa abordagem.

Hoje, apresentamos a entrevista em inglês. A versão em armênio será publicada nos próximos dias.

Site oficial: http://168.am/

Inscreva-se no nosso canal através do seguinte link:    / 168amofficial  
Você também pode seguir a 168.am nas redes sociais:

Facebook:   / 168.am  
Twitter:   / 168am_official

Perhaps Netanyahu, who is a absolutely nasty, delusional man, talked Trump into this. I just wouldn't do that. What kind of games do you want to play? If you want to have an election, keep all foreigners out. keep all the money from the National Endowment for Democracy and the National Republican Institute and the National Democratic Institute and the EU and everyone out. Have an Armenia election, not a foreign election. Don't put Armenia into the center of all these games. God, what kind of security is that? If you want to have observers, ask the secretary general of the UN what to do to help. But the EU, US, or any other party or Russia or anyone else, just stay away. Have an election inside Armenia. You don't need the outside world to do this. Run your own election. And don't allow money coming in from abroad, from any place. just have a domestic election and discuss the main issue, how to remain secure in the South Caucuses without getting into the geopolitical games right now. Not Israel's games, not Donald Trump's games, not anyone else's games. To your mind, what can we face with this kind of foreign policy? Well, I if the invitation is we want to take sides in the current geopolitical games, you will face unfortunately a very difficult future. Rather, this region should say keep us out of the big geopolitics. We just want peace. We want economic development. We want cooperation. We want decent relations with all of our neighbors. We don't want to go to war with anybody. And we don't want to be used by anybody. We are not a pawn of the United States in its transport games between China, Russia, uh Iran, uh so that we provide some game. We are where we are with our neighbors, with our history. Uh and that's where we we're going to be and we're going to live productively with that not foreign policy high stakes we take sides that's the wrong approach professor we have discussed that uh the west is working to sideline Russia and Iran in the south caucuses and parallel we see that um Turkey is substantially strengthening it in its positions in our region in the Middle East and with the growing integration of Turic nations and even there are rumors of Turic army isn't the rise of a neotomantoman empire becoming a problem for the current west all attempts at imperial competition are a problem. We don't have time for these wars these 18th century or 19th century wars. So there are several powers in the world right now. Russia, Turkey in the neighborhood, Iran definitely China and others. Our main goal should not be we're on this side, we're against this one, but rather how do we make peace and stability without stepping on each other's toes, crossing each other's red lines, playing games that threaten each other, putting in whatever kind of infrastructure that says, "Oh, it's good for you, but it hurts them, and it's good for you because it hurts them." If that's the attitude, it's a loser. The infrastructure should be winw win. It should not divide. It should not try to take sides. Taking sides will lose. There are a number of powers and they should uh participate peacefully together. We have also observed a troubling pattern in post-revolutionary states like Georgia, Ukraine, and Armenia. Beyond the political shifts, there is often a systematic campaign uh against traditional institutions like the church. In Armenia, this past year has been dramatic. The government is in an open confrontation with the Armenian Apostolic Church involving criminal cases, arrests, and demands for the Catholicos to step down. Why do these revolutionary movements often target the church? Is this an attempt to dismantle national identity to make to make these nations more malleable in the global uh geopolitical game? Truthfully, I have not followed events in Armenia in this regard, but I have followed events in Ukraine, for example, where the church has been heavily politicized as part of the uh geopolitical and military struggle. And this is tragic. And by the way, uh it leads to the desecration and sometimes even the destruction of uh cathedrals and basilas that date back hundreds or even thousands of years, which is our most precious heritage. So I don't know the specifics but uh people with power try to instrumentalize anything including religion and I don't like governments going after religious leaders and figures and institutions because they're instrumentalizing religion. I don't want the religious uh trying to determine the politics either. So it's a matter of mutual respect and mutual uh uh spheres of activity. Uh I don't know the circumstances in Armenia, but I do see these abuses taking place and they're very harmful. They're very dangerous. People want their culture and their religion for themselves and they don't want this to be instrumentalized in politics and uh general we want to understand geopolitical um developments. Uh do you believe the current breakdown of international order is a symptom of a deeper value crisis where principled policy has been replaced by pure political expediency? Of course uh what we have is a failure of the old uh leadership that is the US and Europe to accept a multi-polar world. The US is trying to maintain its global hegemony. So the US is saying we'll beat the Iranians to a stone age. We'll surround Russia. We'll uh arm Taiwan. We'll invade Venezuela. All to try to prove US power. The US is an empire. Uh it's a particular kind of empire. it uh operates through its military bases all over the world. The goal of the empire is to choose who governs where. Trump wants to choose who governs in Iran. Most places don't want the United States to be the one that determines their politics. National leaders should not succumb to the temptation of saying, "Oh, we love you, Mr. Trump. Please help us. please protect us because this is devastating for countries that behave that way. And so we are in a very difficult situation because we have entered a multipolar world while Europe and the United States are trying to defend their old prerogatives and they thought they had won. Soviet Union ended. Now the United States thought it runs the world and that was its delusion after 1991. Unfortunately uh that delusion has persisted and if anyone watches Donald Trump uh on his speech you can see that not only do we have the national delusion we have a delusional president. What will follow the current stage of global chaos? If politics without a moral foundation ultimately leads to its own destruction, what will eventually fill this vacuum of values? We don't have a vacuum. We have a challenge. We have the UN charter. We have the Universal Declaration of Human Rights. We have the Sustainable Development Goals. We have a a system of international law. The point is it's all under assault. It's all under threat. The United States is abandoning it. So I am not ready for uh to see it all collapse just because the US says so. I am interested in the rest of the world saying no, we're not going to let the US destroy all of this. We are going to continue with international law, with the UN charter, with the human rights council, with many other things. And if one country, the US doesn't want to participate, it won't. But for the rest of us, we're going to go forward. That's how I would like to see things, professor. And as a final thought, we are living through a period of violent global realignment and the redrawing of spheres of influence. And in this new world disorder, what is your fundamental advice to small nations like Armenia? You have already advised very important things, but as a final thought, what is your advice to nations like Armenia, taking into consideration uh geography and all the challenges? My strong advice to all countries is cooperate with your neighbors and uh keep peace and do not be sucked in to great power games. And for Armenia specifically that means that there needs to be cooperation of Armenia, Georgia and Azerbaian as the South Caucus' countries. They need to get along with Russia, with Turkey, with Iran as three regional powers. The South Caucuses should have normal relations with Europe, but also with China, with India, with other uh major powers as well. So the goal should be peace and development with cooperation and avoiding games by the United States or other powers that can drag Armenia into crises that are not of Armenia's interest or making. And the United States is playing games all over the world, very dangerous games, attacking Iran, trying to control the Gulf region, trying to control uh South America, trying to corner China. And for a government the first like the antenna on the head should say we need to be careful not to get into such games. This is the main point. Armenia's real task is help your children get a good education. Uh have good lives. uh protect the beautiful culture of a one of the great civilizations of the world that goes back thousands of years and not to fall into some American delusion about some fight with Israel. I mean Israel's fighting with Iran or some craziness like this or that Armenia is going to be on America's side against China and Russia. Come on. This is absolutely not the right way to go. Be careful and uh be in good contact with neighbors and everybody protect against the outside influences that are angling for advantages but not interested in Armenia's fate. Thank you, professor. Thank you so much for your time. Good to be with you. Appreciate it. And I would like to remind that we were interviewing Professor Jeffrey Sak, global leader in sustainable development, director of the Earth Institute at Colombia University. Don't forget to subscribe to our channel.

videos.title: 'Um discurso sem credibilidade': Prof. Sachs
videos.description:
O discurso do presidente dos EUA, Donald Trump, sobre o Irã foi repleto de bravatas e não de credibilidade, na opinião de Jeffrey Sachs, diretor do Centro para o Desenvolvimento Sustentável da Universidade de Columbia, em conversa exclusiva com Tian Wei, apresentador da CGTN. Na área energética, ele alertou que um grave choque petrolífero já está em curso e pode desencadear uma enorme crise econômica global. #Irã #EUA #JeffreySachs

videos.transcript: Pesquisar transcrição This kind of sentiment is so vulgar and so illegal by the way under international law because countries are not governments are not to say these things under the UN charter. They're not even to threaten the use of force much less to use this kind of force. So, Professor Saxs, you and I are talking right now minutes after the US president gave a national address on the latest situation regarding the war in uh Iran. Now, we understand among the facts that he gives, one is the conflict is likely to last two to three more weeks. What does that mean? I don't think anything is really in Donald Trump's control. The speech was a lot of bravado. Uh the speech was a lot of uh delusion in my view. And during the speech uh the price of oil rose $5 a barrel just in the 25 minutes of the speech. Meaning the markets did not find any of it ex very credible. So I think we have to do what we normally have to do with Trump which is filter these remarks. He said America has won uh two more weeks of bombing Iran back to the stone age and then it will go home. Okay. He also said that the US isn't going to open the stray to formoose. Very interesting. They can't. So if the US has won so decisively, why is that little fact left there? Well, I think basically we have a speech with no credibility by a president with no credibility. Now, Professor Sax, let's go on to another question uh that pretty much being mentioned. The US president argued that the US does not need oil coming from the straight of Hormuz. that's not a concern of the United States because the US pretty much can depend on its own oil. Uh is that true? And how much is US likely to be impacted economically speaking by the ongoing conflict? Well, Americans pay the same price of oil as everybody else. There's a world market for oil. Given that there's a world shortage, the prices of oil and all its derivative products and fertilizer and petrochemicals is soaring in the United States. Americans are, of course, feeling it the same way as everybody else. Uh it may be true that some of our oil companies are making a fortune. Some of Trump's friends may be making a fortune, but the American people are suffering along with everybody else. uh his approval rating is plummeting um and it will continue to plummet. He's a a first class fool as far as I'm concerned. Professor, so you are suggesting that yes indeed the US does not depend on oil coming from Iran or the straight of hormones directly. However, the inflation is a problem for the United States given the fact that the straight of hormones is crucial for all kinds of supply chains around the world. Of course, that's supporting the US consumers. Is that your argument? Well, that's the fact that there's been a decline, a very significant decline of the supply of oil on world markets. Yeah. uh American companies are part of that world supply. When uh Americans buy oil, they're buying oil in an integrated world market. If you look at the price in West Texas, which is our benchmark, and the price of oil in the so-called Brent European pricing, they're basically the same. Not exactly, but they're in one market. Yeah. I don't know if Trump understands any of this, but uh Americans understand at every moment they're suffering from a global uh energy supply side shock that will uh cause the United States and the rest of the world to have a very very serious economic contraction. Right? We also noticed that the US leader in the national address which is shi say the first one of such kind since the conflict began uh compared this latest conflict of war to some of the earlier uh conflicts and wars. He compared that to World War I, two, Korea war, Vietnam war, Iraq war, uh, and suggests it's only 32 days compared to years of, um, wars in the earlier cases. Now, your take on that? Well, it all depends on how long it takes to destroy things. Uh, we're very efficient at destroying things right now. uh the next two weeks could destroy a lot of the energy supplies in the Middle East. Uh in which case uh Mr. Trump's argument isn't going to mean very much. Uh two or three weeks we could see the oil and the gas supplies, the pipelines, the refineries, the gas fields, uh the uh uh energy grids blown up across the region. Trump has threatened again tonight to blow up the energy infrastructure of Iran. Despite his claims, Iran can readily hit uh the neighbors and it has ample missiles, probably thousands to hit uh the neighbors and to hit Israel, by the way. So, there's a lot of bravado. He cannot uh process anything other than complete bravado and declarations of ultimate might and ultimate victory. I don't think what we hear tonight is anything about facts. What we hear is spin. Uh what we hear is Trump's state of mind. We heard basically the US will not open the straight of Hormuz and there will be several more weeks of war and during that period the amount of damage that can be done to the world economy and to the Middle East countries is vast. If uh sensitive targets are hit and I expect that they will be hit in Iran, Iran will retaliate and we will read tomorrow or the next day about the uh unprecedented damage to the Emirates or to Saudi Arabia or to another country across the Gulf. And so we should understand that's why the markets gave a thumbs down within minutes of Trump's remarks. They did not find it credible. I do not find any of it credible. Bravado is not the same thing as truth. Right. It's not fair if we don't mention the other side of the conflict uh in this latest war. Of course, we also hear uh in the news uh with a so-called open letter by the current Iranian president Ken. He in an open letter questioned whether the United States truly pursues so-called an American first policy or merely acts as a proxy for Israel. Also in that same letter he talked about uh that the Israel and he talked about Iran is in no way uh thinking about harming the United States which has been the arguments the US politicians made on one of the reasons why this war need to happen. Now, your immediate takeaway on that as well. I'm afraid he probably underestimates how violent the United States actually is because for the last two years at these meetings at the United Nations, he's been saying, "We want peace. We want negotiation." Uh his country's leaders in return have been murdered by Israel and the United States. So, I I feel sad because this is a letter saying to the American people, we have no animus against you. We don't like to be attacked by your government. But the fact of the matter is that um the United States leadership and Israel's leadership are out to kill and destroy as much of Iran as possible. The president said in his speech uh that they would uh destroy Iran and bring it back to the stone age. This kind of sentiment is so vulgar and so illegal by the way under international law because countries are not governments are not to say these things under the UN charter. They're not even to threaten the use of force much less to use this kind of force. So let's move on to some of the key questions related to the latest conflict and war and what does that mean both for the United States the Iran and also uh for the rest of the world. Uh one of the things is the key the straight of hormones. Now we've seen several occasions, Professor Saxs, that the US president suggested it is not the US goal to control the straight of Hormuz or to occupy the straight of Hormos including this time in the national address. Um so what would that mean for the others? uh how is that going to reconcile with the earlier news leaking out suggesting the US will have the option of taking over uh the uh K island uh and also uh control the straight of Hormuz. So what he says is not dispositive of what the United States is going to do. All we can say is the following. If the US government has decided that it's not an objective to open the straight of form, it will remain under Iranian control. It will remain essentially closed. Whatever bombing they do in the next two or three weeks and whatever reciprocal response Iran gives will not open the stray to form moves. And what it would mean if Trump is reflecting true American policy, which I have to caution again, but if he is, what it means is we are in for an completely massive global economic crisis at the hands of Israel and the United States. That's the bottom line. That's why the oil prices jumped within minutes of Trump's opening remarks as soon as he said what he said. Right. The straight will close. What is not going to happen is Europeans or the Emirates or the Saudis opening the straight for moves. It's not within their power. Period. Iran is on the other side. It can shell the tankers and it is ready to sink the tankers that try to go through. And that's been seen since the start of this operation. It was the single most predictable point of this operation from the very beginning. The other thing is regarding the straight of hormones because it is in everybody's interests um and therefore what are likely to be the impacts of this if the straight of horm continue to be closed or if the straight of hormones is going to be choked either by the United States or by Iran for the others. Professor Sachs it it won't be choked by the United States. It will be closed by Iran as Iran fights for its survival. That's what will happen. And if that happens, there will be a worldwide economic crisis. But given that, what's also true and important to understand is if this war goes on even for weeks, there will be so much destruction of the oil and gas fields and infrastructure, right? That it won't be the straight of Hormuz essentially that's issue. It will be the physical production in the Middle East that will be at issue. In other words, we are going to have a major energy supply reduction. Trump's speech tonight, again, if it can be taken uh at its word, has essentially made clear there will be a phenomenally sharp, probably unprecedented energy crisis. And that we have every reason to expect will create a worldwide economic crisis. Professor, about the oil prices, we have seen the brand price going up to 150 US, which is the highest even higher than the first Gulf War. Uh meanwhile, we also see countries particularly developing and emerging economies cannot afford to have large storage. Some of them even purchase oil on a daily basis. Um so what kind of da disaster we're likely to see for humans on this earth? We've seen uh oil supply shocks twice in the 1970s and the effects were extremely negative, very severe, sharp declines of uh living standards, uh food supply disruptions and this will be no exception. There will be a lot of suffering by people all over the world care of Israel and the United States. This is what is underway. I heard nothing in the speech that gave me much solace about this. the bravado that two or three weeks more we bomb them to the stone age and it's over and we go home and others can clean up our mess is not a uh cogent approach to what is a an a very deep crisis given I I should add something that may seem a little uh subtle or behind beside the point but I it could be very serious There are projections that the weather in the second half of this year especially I mean the climate because of possible serious El Nino uh which is hugely disruptive of food supplies of rainfall patterns of drought right if that comes and compounds this we will have an absolutely unprecedented crisis on our hands right so this the IMF is already giving reports of this. The International Energy Agency has said that the extent of the energy supply shock is unprecedented. Uh and we heard nothing tonight of any intelligent response to this. Not even a concern about it, frankly. Related to that, we've been hearing an orchestra of different voices coming from the region and also from other parts of world whose interests obviously are directly impacted. We've been hearing from the Gulf States. Some have been calling on the United States and Israel to go all the way so to speak while others have been expressing concerns about the latest situations especially the impacts on the region now and in the future. Meanwhile, we've also been hearing from some European governments uh talking about this is quote not their wars and therefore they are not going to be involved. Some of them refused to open the airspace for the US and Israel for their attacks against Iran. Some of them have been in according to media reports in negotiations with Iran regarding the street of Hormuz. But there are a lot of different voices on so many different issues and actually looking at the war in different directions. Now, Professor Saxs, what would this mean? Is the US purposefully pushing everybody into a corner? Uh if they if it is, what does that mean for everybody else? Is this a critical moment for people to choose what kind of world they want? A lot depends on the truth about the military situation. Yeah. The US and Israel say that they have defeated Iran for all intents and purposes, but the skies are open. It's a cleaning up operation and Iran will be back in the stone age. That is their claim. There is another very widespread view that Iran has thousands of missiles and uh much greater numbers of drones uh that it has not been seriously degraded. The US position is that the uh enriched uranium is buried under the ground and monitored by US satellite and no uh issue there. Uh many other people think this is completely preposterous that the Iranians even before the attack last summer remove the enriched uranium to new locations. And I don't know because I'm not in the information chain, but I could say that a lot of the issues that we're discussing depend on the military assessment. Stepping back, I can only make a couple of general points which I would like to make. First, I have never known the US government to tell the truth about the military situation during my lifetime, whether it is Vietnam or whether it is any of the other wars. So, I don't believe a word that is said until it's shown. I do not take the word of the US government at all straightforwardly. Second, a basic principle of war of the last 100 years is that aerial attacks are not sufficient, especially for a country of nearly 100 million people and a land area which is enormous and mountainous and protected and a regime that has known all along that it has two great enemies, the United States and Israel. It has not been the case that a few weeks of bombing has ever ended a war like this. So on general principles though I am not any kind of definitive expert and I have zero inside information on this. I do not believe the US narrative and many of the things that we're discussing will come down to who is right and wrong about the military situation. I think it is likely that Iran has very substantial military power, missile systems, hypersonic missile systems, the ability to cause grave damage across the Gulf and in Israel. Yeah. And that the US claim that this is not the case is will be proven to be false. If that is right, we are in a completely new world situation. If that conclusion is correct, if the United States is right in what President Trump said, then Iran will have been brutally subjected to aggression, but it will not change fundamentally the world balance. So unfortunately there is no answer right now. Right. uh that that is clear. I believe that the weight of the evidence right now is that uh Trump is uh blowing a lot of hot air uh and as usual the United States government is not being straightforward about the real situation.

videos.title: 'Não esperem por uma guerra mundial. Salvem a ONU agora': alerta o Prof. Sachs.
videos.description:
O professor Jeffrey Sachs, da Universidade Columbia, argumenta que a ordem mundial atual é movida menos por regras do que pelo poder bruto. Em sua visão, o que existe é, na prática, um império americano – um império que não respeita mais o direito internacional. Ele alerta que as Nações Unidas enfrentam desafios crescentes, já que seu país fundador se afastou consideravelmente da organização. Sua mensagem para o resto do mundo é clara: não esperem por outra guerra mundial e ajam agora para preservar a ONU. #Irã #EUA #JeffreySachs #ONU
