videos.transcript: Pesquisar transcrição The United States is an empire. It has its empire is reflected in the military bases around the world in Asia, in Korea, in Japan. They've been there for 80 years after all. 80 years over the past decades about the US being the leader of the free world. Now, of course, that is a political slogan. Some can even say it. It is really a propaganda slogan. But on the other hand, is there something called the free world? No. There is something called the world in which America has military bases. There are about 80 countries in the world where the United States has military bases. That is the American Empire. And there are roughly 750 US military bases in around 80 countries. And those countries don't really breathe. When you host an American military base, you give up your sovereignty. That's what's happened to Bahrain, to the Emirates, to Qatar, to Saudi Arabia. And they did that because they thought that is protection for them. It's not protection. It is occupation. And so we don't have clear thought in any part of the world but free world well freedom of US bombing missions that's the freedom uh freedom to break any international law but these slogans mean nothing right now they don't characterize governments they don't characterize behavior they don't care characterize lawfulness I do a study each year on which countries are most aligned with the UN charter. There are 193 UN member states. The United States comes out last on objective criteria. Last. Not at the bottom. Not in the bottom 10%. Not in the bottom 20%. 193rd. The US obeys no international law right now. So what kind of order are we talking about? What we're talking about is force. The United States is an empire. It has its empire is reflected in the military bases around the world in Asia, in Korea, in Japan. They've been there for 80 years after all in 80 years. So that's the empire. When you are betting on that empire, you're betting on the US side of things. The fact of the matter is from the point of view of technology and innovation and markets and trade that world doesn't even exist anymore. Right? The BRICS country, the BRICS countries are much larger than the G7 countries in economic size. China is ahead of the United States in innovation across huge swaths of the economy right now. So still the bases are there and when you watch government behavior of governments that host US military bases it's a little bit like vantriloquism. to hear the US words coming out of the mouths of the leaders of these countries because to host a US military base is to give up your sovereignty to an important right the meanwhile I would like to follow up on a few points professor Saxs you just made I I would love to hear more of your in-depth uh analysis uh to say the least the US role has been recalibrated in terms of global governance that's for sure uh to what degree Well, we're going to observe over the next few weeks and months probably uh even more clearly as you argued. But professor, the US did help to create and reinforce over the past the decades since second world war some of the international institutions the United Nations, IMF, Breton Wood, NATO and some of the list goes on. So how much do you think the value of this different entities, mechanisms and structures should be devalued in a way as a result of the US which is one of the key creators of this system are not believing in this system to begin with in your argument and now certainly want to leave all these international governance structures. So um how much do we still need to value these structures? That's an interesting question, isn't it? And a and a logical question. And my recommendation for the rest of the world, which is somewhat in shock about this, is don't wait for World War II to create a new organization. Save this one. Yeah. And my recommendation for China, very strong recommendation is make a major UN campus in Beijing because there is no major UN organization in Asia. It was born in the United States or in Europe. This is where almost all of the UN agencies are. But Asia is home to more than it's 60% of the world population. True. Half the world output. And so we need to support the UN. You know, the UN is the second attempt. The League of Nations was the first attempt. It failed. It failed and it died in World War II. Now we have the UN. It's hanging by a thread because the host country, the inventor of it, has abandoned it to a large extent. And so the other 190 plus countries need to ask, what are we going to do? Let the United States flush this down the drain or save it. My advice is to save it because World War II is not an option for us. It is not the right approach to say we'll get the next one after the next world war just like the past one. We need to save this. And we desperately need leaders to be saying to rogue nations, and we have two of them right now, the United States and Israel, you cannot do what you're doing. A president of the United States cannot give a speech declaring that they're going to bomb Iran back to the stone age. This has to be unacceptable on its face. It has to have a reaction from governments immediately. Not hm wonder if he's going to bomb them back to the stone age, but rather that is completely unacceptable in the 21st century in a nuclear age. We cannot even have that kind of talk much less that kind of intention. Right. Professor, my last questions related to uh the fear that some have been suggesting if things are not being managed well regarding the conflict among Israel, United States and Iran. Of course, rippling effect, we are likely to be on the verge of a another world war. Well, that is maybe an exaggeration or it might be a clear warning. Uh what do you make of the latest situation and how much uh roles others could play in prevent the things from further escalating into something that nobody wants to see. We're on the verge of world war because of many dangerous delusional ideas right now mainly in the United States. The US has essentially declared it runs the Western Hemisphere. It says that's the Monroe Doctrine. The Monroe Doctrine had nothing to do with that. The Monroe Doctrine was only a warning to Europe. Do not colonize the Western Hemisphere. It was not a claim by the United States to dominate the Western Hemisphere. So the mindset right now is very very dangerous and the mindset of the US is extremely dangerous because it's built on a fundamental delusion that the United States can do what it wants. But if we continue this way there will be wars breaking out everywhere. And interestingly, by the way, I think it's important to say with all the US attack on the evil of Iran, how many wars has Iran launched in the last decade? Zero. In the last 40 years, zero. In the last 80 years, zero. In the last century, zero. Iran has not been a country launching wars. How many has the United States launched? Nearly every year some kind of new military adventure. How do you see this part of the world? Well, you have a conflict going on and it's ripple effect likely to show the true colors of a lot of logics in the world and likely to change the world to certain extent. You also have other players interacting with the United States in different ways. Um, and China, United States is only just one of those example. You also had the EU or the Gulf regions and Latin America and the list goes on. So, how do you see this many moving factors? Uh, what kind of momentum do you read out of this? Well, you know, chi China has a very stable, mature and careful state craft. So uh the Chinese government doesn't see any reason to provoke the United States in areas of trade uh finance uh and related topics and so it's trying to have uh some kind of stability. We saw that in those areas too however Trump had his reciprocal tariffs and his trade wars and his threats and uh everything else. So there's no real stability there as well. As I read the situation, uh, China and, uh, others are trying to have as, uh, non-provocative relations as possible in these areas, but without illusions. Uh, not believing the United States is a stable country or even or much less a stable partner. To believe so would be a huge mistake. China's stable partners are especially the emerging economies of the world. They need what China has. China trades with them. China has good diplomacy with them. And I think the idea is don't rock the boat for no reason. But the truth of the matter is it's also not possible to duck the war and peace issues. China has an enormous role and stake in ending this war in the Gulf just as one of the world's great powers and one of the world's responsible powers. Uh and of course a leader in the United Nations, Russia has the same responsibility. Uh India though it's not a UN security council member has a very big responsibility. So in addition to trying to have uh a state visit perhaps and uh discussions about trade that that don't blow up, the truth of the matter is uh the major powers and I'm again always coming back to China, to Russia, to the BRICS countries really do need to help explain to Mr. Trump and to the United States. You cannot have wars to bomb countries to the stone age in the 21st century. The idea, the approach, the rhetoric can get us all killed and it has to stop. Do you see the danger about future attacks on Cuba or Greenland? Of course. And the list goes on. Of course. Of course. They're absolutely going to invade Cuba one day. They're going to declare Greenland part of US territory one day. This is the mindset right now. Grab what you can. They're absolutely going to do this. Jeffrey Sachs, professor, always a pleasure. Thank you so much for your straightforwardness and insightful ideas. Appreciate it. Great to be with you. Thank you.

videos.title: O Irã está em vantagem na guerra: a Armênia vai explodir, não se deixem arrastar para a estupidez...
videos.description:
Inscreva-se no canal do YouTube da 168.am e acompanhe as notícias, entrevistas e análises constantemente atualizadas da 168.am.

#Notícias #DaArmênia #NOTÍCIAS #168 #168am #Armênia #168TV

#ao vivo #notícias #168jam #Armênia #novostiArmenii #Novosti_Armenii_segodnya #Políticas_Armênias

#JeffreySachs #168TV #AraksMartirosyan #EntrevistaExclusiva #168am #Diplomata #Armênia #Irã #EUA #Israel #Geopolítica #PolíticaExterna #CáucasoDoSul #OrienteMédio #RelaçõesInternacionais #SegurançaRegional
#EUArmênia #RelaçõesRusso-Armênias #Trump2026 #GuerraHíbrida #DesenvolvimentoSustentável #TendênciasGlobais #EleiçõesParlamentares #AgendaDeSegurança
O convidado exclusivo de hoje no programa “Diplomat” da 168TV é o economista de renome mundial e autoridade líder em desenvolvimento sustentável, Jeffrey Sachs, Diretor do Instituto da Terra da Universidade de Columbia.

Em uma entrevista abrangente com o jornalista Araks Martirosyan, da 168.am, o Professor Sachs examina as principais tendências políticas globais, oferecendo uma avaliação completa do confronto entre os EUA e Israel com o Irã e delineando possíveis cenários finais, incluindo o papel dos mediadores internacionais. Ele também aborda aspectos-chave da agenda de política externa da Armênia, incluindo as relações Armênia-EUA, o “caminho de Trump”, a dinâmica armênio-russa e as perspectivas para a potencial adesão da Armênia à UE. Além disso, Sachs avalia os esforços do governo armênio para combater as ameaças híbridas nas próximas eleições parlamentares em cooperação com a UE, bem como as potenciais implicações dessa abordagem.

Hoje, apresentamos a entrevista em inglês. A versão em armênio será publicada nos próximos dias.

Site oficial: http://168.am/

Inscreva-se no nosso canal através do seguinte link:    / 168amofficial  
Você também pode seguir a 168.am nas redes sociais:

Facebook:   / 168.am  
Twitter:   / 168am_official
videos.transcript: Pesquisar transcrição Welcome. It's 168 Armenian TV. I am Rx Martian and this is Diplomat. Today I am honored to interview Professor Jeffrey Sak, world known economist, global leader in sustainable development, director of Earth Institute at Columbia University. Professor, thank you so much for your time. My pleasure. Great to be with you. Thank you, professor. We must address the monthslong war against Iran right on Armenia's doorstep. Given the US national security strategy released this past December, it was hard to imagine Washington getting dragged into such an exhausting war against Iran. The understanding was that Trump would pursue a policy of no more forever wars and uh place less emphasis on a militarized foreign policy. Uh how did we get here and where is the US foreign policy and this war headed now? Excellent question. Uh and uh we in America are scratching our heads as well because uh the fact of the matter is Trump lied brazenly to his own people uh to his political promises. He campaigned on the basis that he would not do exactly what he is doing right now. And he is creating chaos. he and Netanyahu together. Uh this Israel US uh war of aggression on Iran is threatening the entire world, the world economy and the global security. So this is a war without reason. Uh it's been called a war of choice, but a better term is also used a war of whim. In other words, Trump and Hegsath and a few people said, "Okay, we'll do this." Perhaps Netanyahu, who is a absolutely nasty, delusional man, talked Trump into this. What's clear is that there was no careful process inside the United States government, no review of options, no considering the military and the economic fallout of this. And so this is a war chosen by Donald Trump. It is not in America's interest. It is not in this case driven by uh anything other than grandiose delusions of power that Trump and Netanyahu have. I don't think in this case it was pushed uh so much by the deep state though the idea of US hegemony is always part of the deep state agenda. This is a war chosen primarily by a small group. I would even say a small gang because it is outside of American law. It is outside of international law. It is creating a global crisis and everybody is watching from inside the United States against this and internationally against this. So it's a a very good question that you raised and nobody has given a a definitive answer to this but we can see that Trump has chosen this. What is the strategy of Trump's policy in this war? The strategy is to try to kill as many Iranian people as possible, to destroy the Iranian government, to destroy the infrastructure, and to put it in Trump's terms, to drive Iran back to the stone age. This is rather primitive. Uh this is rather vulgar. This is not diplomacy, strategy, international law, even US law. This is a primitive, probably demented view of a psychopath, I would say, who is in charge in the US together with a psychopath who is in charge in Israel. But that's their goal. Trump explained it clearly yesterday in his speech. The goal is to drive Iran back to the stone age. Professor, looking at the battlefield and the regional dynamics, who has the upper hand uh now in the conflict, in the war? If you look at what the markets say, they say Iran. Uh because as President Trump was speaking, oil prices were soaring. uh and uh that means that the markets believe that the disruption of the world energy will persist and rather than will be solved quickly and so the upper hand uh is with Iran according to the markets according to the military specialists of course there is a divided opinion but many military specialists believe that Iran can uh basically outlast the United States because each drone is maybe $20,000, but each uh missile interceptor can be $2 million. And so this is not a case where defense is actually going to uh dominate, but where offense is. And then that raises the question, does Iran have a lasting offensive capacity in drones and missile systems? Again, there's a a debate. If you listen to Trump, he says we have obliterated their capacity. If you listen to most military experts, they say that Iran continues to have vast stockpiles protected underground in bunkers of missiles and drones and the ability to deliver them. So, I don't think there's a definitive answer. We're going to find out the hard way in the next few weeks. Does the US crush Iran? As vulgar and illegal as that is, it still is a question, is it feasible from the US point of view, short of nuclear weapons, of course. Uh, and probably the answer is no. Probably Iran has major retaliatory power and will use it. Professor, uh, reports from the Pentagon indicate that Washington may be gearing up for a ground invasion of Iran with the projected timeline of several weeks. Is there any strategic gain that justifies such a massive risk if the war goes on for several weeks? My guess, again, I'm guessing and would really like to know more than perhaps is possible to know, but my guess is that a great deal of the energy systems in the Persian Gulf region will be destroyed by then. So even a ground invasion will be a rather moot question from the point of view of global security and global economy. My view is that if the war goes on for a few weeks and Israel and the United States continue to behave the way they're behaving and continue to accumulate troops, we will have an irreversible worldwide energy calamity which will mean a very deep economic crisis for years to come. it would just be too late to ask the question about opening the straight of form moves from the point of view of what a ground invasion could accomplish. Of course, the United States does not have the means to have a ground uh invasion that actually conquers Iran. Iran is a vast country, a mountainous country, a difficult country with a large army. Uh the number of troops coming from the United States is 20 or 30,000 perhaps. It's not small, but it is not large enough to invade Iran. Maybe their idea is that they capture the coastline to control the straight of form. We don't know because they don't tell us and uh what they do tell us are lies, not the truth. Uh anyway, but maybe they're looking for a limited operation. All I can say is again as a uh as an economist not a military expert from what I hear from experts even such a limited operation would be bloody and extraordinarily difficult professor what will stop the war to your mind to my mind a few grown-ups explaining to Mr. Trump reality. Uh the grown-ups are President Xi, Prime Minister Modi, President Putin. Maybe uh if there were or if there are grown-ups in Europe, the only one I know right now is President Sanchez of Spain, but a group of world leaders explaining to Mr. Trump, your delusions of grandeur are pushing the world to the brink and we do not accept that and you need to stop. And now about the South Caucuses. How will the war against Iran reshape the South Caucuses? We are already witnessing a clear push by the West to displace Russian and Iranian influence from our region. And in this process, a key element now is the proposed Trump road uh through Armenian territory under uh significant US control given this situation. Uh what are the primary risks to your mind for Armenia? Very simply, anyone that signs up with Mr. Trump should have his head examined right now. If you want to sign up with Mr. Trump, go talk to Bahrain. Go talk to Qatar. Go talk to the Emirates. Go talk to the Saudis. Ask, "Is that really a very good idea? Did the US protect you? Does having something called a Trump highway or Trump project, is that really uh helpful for you right now? It's a crazy idea. I'm sorry to say as bluntly as I can, stay away from American wars. This is not the business for the South Caucuses to get pulled into America's and Israel's craziness. Please stay safe. Stay outside of this craziness. America's playing games with the rest of the world. It thinks it owns Latin America. It thinks it controls the Middle East. It wants to play games in the caucuses. The caucuses are absolutely magnificent, beautiful places of 10,000 years of culture. Don't get pulled into the US stupidity because you'll end up getting blown up and something called a Trump anything right now. Watch Mr. Trump yesterday and then ask, "Is that the man you're signing up with?" Because it shouldn't be. This is very clear. The caucuses are where they are. You need good relationship within the caucuses. I know it's hard, but it's important. And then with Russia, with Turkey, with Iran, those are your neighbors. You let the United States in, you're just inviting war. Please don't do that. The US doesn't care about war on your territory. It doesn't care about war in Bahrain or in Qatar or in the Emirates. Trump said so. We're gone. You guys fix it. Don't let this happen to your magnificent part of the world. Professor, our authorities insist that this partnership with United States and agreement around Trump road will guarantee existence of Armenian Republic 100 years. What you think about this? I think that this is very sad. Very very sad. Armenia, first of all, needs to exist for thousands of years. Armenia has existed for thousands of years. Armenia is not going to find security signing up with Donald Trump. Please look seriously at yesterday's speech and just ask the question, is that your partner? The answer you should figure out. But I will tell you my view. The answer is no. And I've said it. I said it to the Gulf leaders months ago. Do not trust this. You will get blown up your economy, your security. The United States is a rogue nation right now. What kind of partner talks about putting Iran to the stone age? That's Armenia's safety to have that partner, the one that said yesterday, we're going to put Iran, I'm sorry, Iran to the stone age. Let me say that correctly. That's the partner for Armenia. Do you know how risky that is? And you think the United States is deeply dedicated to Armenia's security? Are you kidding? There aren't five non-Armenian Americans that know where Armenia is in the world and including Donald Trump. He doesn't know. He doesn't care. If your authorities say that this is security to sign up to some Trump highway, oh my god, come on. Pay attention. This is not security. Don't get into American geopolitical games. Please. You're much too beautiful a country, much too vulnerable a country to get into America's geopolitical games. Every day I repeat Henry Kissinger's famous adage that to be an enemy of the United States is dangerous, but to be a friend is fatal. Take heed. Please be careful. And anything called Trump anything is by itself a sign. You've got somehow the wrong calculation here given the strategic partnership between Azarban and Israel and the ongoing discussions about Azarbajan's potential role in a confrontation with Iran. Is it possible that Armenia could also face external pressure to take steps against Iran? Let me just make a basic point, another very basic point. Israel has committed a genocide in Gaza before our eyes. Mass murder of innocent people. Can that be a partner for Azerbaijan? Possibly. Please everybody stay out of trouble because this is not safe. And Mr. Netanyahu said he cast the 10 uh plagues on Iran. What does he think he's God? This is not safe. These civilizational wars are not safe. Don't get into them. They're very dangerous. stay out of wars and stay out of these absolutely unstable and dangerous situations. I'm speaking to Azerbaijan. Who needs to be blown up right now? Who needs to be part of a wider war? Stay clear of Israel and the United States in this. Just keep your neutrality. sell your oil to world markets. There's a good price for it now because of uh the US uh destruction, but stay away from these entanglements. I tried to explain to the Emirates. Why do you join this Abraham Accord with a country committing a genocide in Gaza? Well, they said, "Oh, but this is strategic for us." Well, how strategic is it now for the Emirates, which is the whole economy is based on peace, stability, tourism, safety of bringing money uh to the financial markets and so forth. What's happened? The hotels are getting bombed. The ports are getting bombed. The oil doesn't transit because of a regional war. South Caucuses, please stay out of war. This is the main point. Professor, you insist that we must stay away from the war from the United States. But uh the prime minister of Armenia met yesterday with Putin and uh they discussed Armenia's possible move toward the EU despite the fact that Armenia hasn't been invited to join European Union. What do you think is Armenia expected in the EU? And what risks uh could this pose for Armenia? Countries that don't understand their own geography, that play geopolitical games end up paying a big price. This is Ukraine's biggest mistake. They said, "Oh, we can do what we want. We can join NATO." And they ended up getting into a devastating, predictable war. If you look at a map, Ukraine should be neutral, then it would be safe. And that's how it was with Mr. Yanukovich until he was ousted in a coup that was backed by the United States. My advice really for the South Caucuses is understand the South Caucuses have a distinct, wonderful history and geography. According to my map in Asia, not in Europe. My understanding is that below the crest of the Grand Caucuses, mountain ridge, this is Asia. Uh and um but most importantly be careful. Please. The South Caucuses should be together. They should understand uh everyone wants to come visit for the tourism, for the beauty, for the ancient heritage, for the uh ancient churches, for the great art, for the uh the wines, you name it. Uh they don't want to come visit a war zone. They don't want to come to greater Israel. They don't want to be part of a Trump something or other. uh they want to come to the South Caucuses. And your neighbors for thousands of years of history, by the way, uh are uh uh not Israel or the United States, I have to say, but your neighbors, Russia, Turkey, uh Iran, uh and others. And the outside world should have some decent respect and distance. And I'm sorry if the government is a little bit behind the time uh and thinking somehow that the US is the world hegeimon uh and that you side with the US you get security and that Europe is in great shape so that we just want to join the European Union for what it barely can take one step and it's going economically down because the EU has done two fundamentally wrong things. One is to break its links with Russia which is absurd because they are perfectly complimentary economies. And second, Europe has become a defense pact. Europe was once an economic pact, but now it's all about security. So if Armenia says we want to join the European Union, that's saying we want to join the European Security Alliance. That can't make your neighbors feel very comfortable about that either. So I'm afraid the government is 10 or 20 years behind. Even then, I would have disagreed with it. But look at today's reality. Do you really want to march to war? I wouldn't. I would stay clear of war. I would stay clear of this kind of geopolitical tension. I would try to find arrangements that are congenial within the neighborhood because that's who you have to live with. Moreover, I must mention that Armenia is heading toward elections and the current administration has invited the EU to help counter what they called hybrid threats from Russia. Given your experience with global power dynamics, do you see this as a stabilizing move or it's inviting further geopolitical confrontation into Armenia's internal politics? Boy, I wouldn't do that. I just wouldn't do that. What kind of games do you want to play? If you want to have an election, keep all foreigners out. keep all the money from the National Endowment for Democracy and the National Republican Institute and the National Democratic Institute and the EU and everyone out. Have an Armenia election, not a foreign election. Don't put Armenia into the center of all these games. God, what kind of security is that? If you want to have observers, ask the secretary general of the UN what to do to help. But the EU, US, or any other party or Russia or anyone else, just stay away. Have an election inside Armenia. You don't need the outside world to do this. Run your own election. And don't allow money coming in from abroad, from any place. just have a domestic election and discuss the main issue, how to remain secure in the South Caucuses without getting into the geopolitical games right now. Not Israel's games, not Donald Trump's games, not anyone else's games. To your mind, what can we face with this kind of foreign policy? Well, I if the invitation is we want to take sides in the current geopolitical games, you will face unfortunately a very difficult future. Rather, this region should say keep us out of the big geopolitics. We just want peace. We want economic development. We want cooperation. We want decent relations with all of our neighbors. We don't want to go to war with anybody. And we don't want to be used by anybody. We are not a pawn of the United States in its transport games between China, Russia, uh Iran, uh so that we provide some game. We are where we are with our neighbors, with our history. Uh and that's where we we're going to be and we're going to live productively with that not foreign policy high stakes we take sides that's the wrong approach professor we have discussed that uh the west is working to sideline Russia and Iran in the south caucuses and parallel we see that um Turkey is substantially strengthening it in its positions in our region in the Middle East and with the growing integration of Turic nations and even there are rumors of Turic army isn't the rise of a neotomantoman empire becoming a problem for the current west all attempts at imperial competition are a problem. We don't have time for these wars these 18th century or 19th century wars. So there are several powers in the world right now. Russia, Turkey in the neighborhood, Iran definitely China and others. Our main goal should not be we're on this side, we're against this one, but rather how do we make peace and stability without stepping on each other's toes, crossing each other's red lines, playing games that threaten each other, putting in whatever kind of infrastructure that says, "Oh, it's good for you, but it hurts them, and it's good for you because it hurts them." If that's the attitude, it's a loser. The infrastructure should be winw win. It should not divide. It should not try to take sides. Taking sides will lose. There are a number of powers and they should uh participate peacefully together. We have also observed a troubling pattern in post-revolutionary states like Georgia, Ukraine, and Armenia. Beyond the political shifts, there is often a systematic campaign uh against traditional institutions like the church. In Armenia, this past year has been dramatic. The government is in an open confrontation with the Armenian Apostolic Church involving criminal cases, arrests, and demands for the Catholicos to step down. Why do these revolutionary movements often target the church? Is this an attempt to dismantle national identity to make to make these nations more malleable in the global uh geopolitical game? Truthfully, I have not followed events in Armenia in this regard, but I have followed events in Ukraine, for example, where the church has been heavily politicized as part of the uh geopolitical and military struggle. And this is tragic. And by the way, uh it leads to the desecration and sometimes even the destruction of uh cathedrals and basilas that date back hundreds or even thousands of years, which is our most precious heritage. So I don't know the specifics but uh people with power try to instrumentalize anything including religion and I don't like governments going after religious leaders and figures and institutions because they're instrumentalizing religion. I don't want the religious uh trying to determine the politics either. So it's a matter of mutual respect and mutual uh uh spheres of activity. Uh I don't know the circumstances in Armenia, but I do see these abuses taking place and they're very harmful. They're very dangerous. People want their culture and their religion for themselves and they don't want this to be instrumentalized in politics and uh general we want to understand geopolitical um developments. Uh do you believe the current breakdown of international order is a symptom of a deeper value crisis where principled policy has been replaced by pure political expediency? Of course uh what we have is a failure of the old uh leadership that is the US and Europe to accept a multi-polar world. The US is trying to maintain its global hegemony. So the US is saying we'll beat the Iranians to a stone age. We'll surround Russia. We'll uh arm Taiwan. We'll invade Venezuela. All to try to prove US power. The US is an empire. Uh it's a particular kind of empire. it uh operates through its military bases all over the world. The goal of the empire is to choose who governs where. Trump wants to choose who governs in Iran. Most places don't want the United States to be the one that determines their politics. National leaders should not succumb to the temptation of saying, "Oh, we love you, Mr. Trump. Please help us. please protect us because this is devastating for countries that behave that way. And so we are in a very difficult situation because we have entered a multipolar world while Europe and the United States are trying to defend their old prerogatives and they thought they had won. Soviet Union ended. Now the United States thought it runs the world and that was its delusion after 1991. Unfortunately uh that delusion has persisted and if anyone watches Donald Trump uh on his speech you can see that not only do we have the national delusion we have a delusional president. What will follow the current stage of global chaos? If politics without a moral foundation ultimately leads to its own destruction, what will eventually fill this vacuum of values? We don't have a vacuum. We have a challenge. We have the UN charter. We have the Universal Declaration of Human Rights. We have the Sustainable Development Goals. We have a a system of international law. The point is it's all under assault. It's all under threat. The United States is abandoning it. So I am not ready for uh to see it all collapse just because the US says so. I am interested in the rest of the world saying no, we're not going to let the US destroy all of this. We are going to continue with international law, with the UN charter, with the human rights council, with many other things. And if one country, the US doesn't want to participate, it won't. But for the rest of us, we're going to go forward. That's how I would like to see things, professor. And as a final thought, we are living through a period of violent global realignment and the redrawing of spheres of influence. And in this new world disorder, what is your fundamental advice to small nations like Armenia? You have already advised very important things, but as a final thought, what is your advice to nations like Armenia, taking into consideration uh geography and all the challenges? My strong advice to all countries is cooperate with your neighbors and uh keep peace and do not be sucked in to great power games. And for Armenia specifically that means that there needs to be cooperation of Armenia, Georgia and Azerbaian as the South Caucus' countries. They need to get along with Russia, with Turkey, with Iran as three regional powers. The South Caucuses should have normal relations with Europe, but also with China, with India, with other uh major powers as well. So the goal should be peace and development with cooperation and avoiding games by the United States or other powers that can drag Armenia into crises that are not of Armenia's interest or making. And the United States is playing games all over the world, very dangerous games, attacking Iran, trying to control the Gulf region, trying to control uh South America, trying to corner China. And for a government the first like the antenna on the head should say we need to be careful not to get into such games. This is the main point. Armenia's real task is help your children get a good education. Uh have good lives. uh protect the beautiful culture of a one of the great civilizations of the world that goes back thousands of years and not to fall into some American delusion about some fight with Israel. I mean Israel's fighting with Iran or some craziness like this or that Armenia is going to be on America's side against China and Russia. Come on. This is absolutely not the right way to go. Be careful and uh be in good contact with neighbors and everybody protect against the outside influences that are angling for advantages but not interested in Armenia's fate. Thank you, professor. Thank you so much for your time. Good to be with you. Appreciate it. And I would like to remind that we were interviewing Professor Jeffrey Sak, global leader in sustainable development, director of the Earth Institute at Colombia University. Don't forget to subscribe to our channel.

videos.title: 'Um discurso sem credibilidade': Prof. Sachs
videos.description:
O discurso do presidente dos EUA, Donald Trump, sobre o Irã foi repleto de bravatas e não de credibilidade, na opinião de Jeffrey Sachs, diretor do Centro para o Desenvolvimento Sustentável da Universidade de Columbia, em conversa exclusiva com Tian Wei, apresentador da CGTN. Na área energética, ele alertou que um grave choque petrolífero já está em curso e pode desencadear uma enorme crise econômica global. #Irã #EUA #JeffreySachs

📺 Inscreva-se no nosso canal do YouTube e fique por dentro das nossas análises e entrevistas mais recentes: 👇
   / @cgtn   👈

Baixe nosso aplicativo na Apple Store (iOS): https://itunes.apple.com/us/app/cctvn...
Baixe nosso aplicativo no Google Play (Android): https://play.google.com/store/apps/de...

Para mais informações:
https://www.cgtn.com/video
videos.transcript: Pesquisar transcrição This kind of sentiment is so vulgar and so illegal by the way under international law because countries are not governments are not to say these things under the UN charter. They're not even to threaten the use of force much less to use this kind of force. So, Professor Saxs, you and I are talking right now minutes after the US president gave a national address on the latest situation regarding the war in uh Iran. Now, we understand among the facts that he gives, one is the conflict is likely to last two to three more weeks. What does that mean? I don't think anything is really in Donald Trump's control. The speech was a lot of bravado. Uh the speech was a lot of uh delusion in my view. And during the speech uh the price of oil rose $5 a barrel just in the 25 minutes of the speech. Meaning the markets did not find any of it ex very credible. So I think we have to do what we normally have to do with Trump which is filter these remarks. He said America has won uh two more weeks of bombing Iran back to the stone age and then it will go home. Okay. He also said that the US isn't going to open the stray to formoose. Very interesting. They can't. So if the US has won so decisively, why is that little fact left there? Well, I think basically we have a speech with no credibility by a president with no credibility. Now, Professor Sax, let's go on to another question uh that pretty much being mentioned. The US president argued that the US does not need oil coming from the straight of Hormuz. that's not a concern of the United States because the US pretty much can depend on its own oil. Uh is that true? And how much is US likely to be impacted economically speaking by the ongoing conflict? Well, Americans pay the same price of oil as everybody else. There's a world market for oil. Given that there's a world shortage, the prices of oil and all its derivative products and fertilizer and petrochemicals is soaring in the United States. Americans are, of course, feeling it the same way as everybody else. Uh it may be true that some of our oil companies are making a fortune. Some of Trump's friends may be making a fortune, but the American people are suffering along with everybody else. uh his approval rating is plummeting um and it will continue to plummet. He's a a first class fool as far as I'm concerned. Professor, so you are suggesting that yes indeed the US does not depend on oil coming from Iran or the straight of hormones directly. However, the inflation is a problem for the United States given the fact that the straight of hormones is crucial for all kinds of supply chains around the world. Of course, that's supporting the US consumers. Is that your argument? Well, that's the fact that there's been a decline, a very significant decline of the supply of oil on world markets. Yeah. uh American companies are part of that world supply. When uh Americans buy oil, they're buying oil in an integrated world market. If you look at the price in West Texas, which is our benchmark, and the price of oil in the so-called Brent European pricing, they're basically the same. Not exactly, but they're in one market. Yeah. I don't know if Trump understands any of this, but uh Americans understand at every moment they're suffering from a global uh energy supply side shock that will uh cause the United States and the rest of the world to have a very very serious economic contraction. Right? We also noticed that the US leader in the national address which is shi say the first one of such kind since the conflict began uh compared this latest conflict of war to some of the earlier uh conflicts and wars. He compared that to World War I, two, Korea war, Vietnam war, Iraq war, uh, and suggests it's only 32 days compared to years of, um, wars in the earlier cases. Now, your take on that? Well, it all depends on how long it takes to destroy things. videos.title: Irã divulga imagens bombásticas de helicóptero CH-47 Chinook americano destruído após impacto dir...
videos.description:
A mídia estatal iraniana divulgou imagens não verificadas alegando que um ataque com drone danificou um helicóptero CH-47 Chinook dos EUA no Kuwait. Relatos sugerem que a aeronave fazia parte de uma missão relacionada à queda de um caça F-15E Strike Eagle dos EUA em território iraniano. Um membro da tripulação teria sido resgatado, enquanto outro permanece desaparecido, e as operações de busca continuam. O Pentágono não confirmou as alegações. Com o aumento das tensões, o presidente dos EUA, Donald Trump, classificou a situação como "guerra", sinalizando que não haverá trégua nas hostilidades. Os acontecimentos representam uma escalada perigosa no conflito em curso entre Irã e EUA.

#GuerraIrãEUA #AtaqueAoChinook #F15Abatido #ÚltimasNotícias #ConflitoNoOrienteMédio #ForçasMilitarEUA #AtaqueAoIrã #AlertaDeGuerra #Geopolítica #Trump

O Times of India (TOI) é o jornal diário em inglês mais vendido do mundo.

Os vídeos do Times of India trazem notícias globais, opiniões e análises precisas. Acompanhamos a ascensão global da Índia, seu crescente engajamento com o mundo, o cenário geopolítico em transformação em meio a conflitos e guerras e a ordem mundial emergente.

NOTÍCIAS INTERNACIONAIS | CONFLITOS GLOBAIS | GUERRA NO ORIENTE MÉDIO | MUDANÇAS NA ORDEM MUNDIAL #TOILive | #TOIVideos

Inscreva-se no canal do Times of India no YouTube e clique no ícone do sino para receber notificações quando entrarmos ao vivo.

Junte-se a milhões de leitores do TOI; baixe nosso aplicativo em http://toi.in/app-yt

Visite nosso site: https://www.timesofindia.com/
Siga-nos no X/ @timesofindia
Facebook:   / timesofindia  
Instagram:   / timesofindia  
Siga o canal do TOI no WhatsApp: https://bit.ly/3RYl0J9
Para notícias e atualizações diárias e reportagens exclusivas, siga o Times of India
videos.transcript: Pesquisar transcrição A dramatic escalation in the Iran US conflict. Unverified images now claim a direct Iranian strike on a US military helicopter in Q8. Iranian state media outlet Press TV has released these images purportedly showing a damaged US Army CH47 Shinook at Camp Buring, a key American military hub in the Gulf. According to Iranian reports, a drone strike hit the aircraft, leaving the heavy lift helicopter severely damaged. The Shinook is a critical asset used to move troops, weapons, and supplies across the region. But there is a major catch. There is no official confirmation yet from Washington. The Pentagon has remained silent so far. Another Iranian outlet claims something even more striking. It says the helicopter was part of a search operation looking for a downed US fighter jet pilot. Because behind this incident lies an even bigger story. A US F-15E Strike Eagle has been shot down over Iran, marking a major turning point in the conflict. According to reports, one crew member has been rescued alive and now in US custody, receiving medical treatment, but the second crew member is still missing. Search and rescue operations are ongoing and time is critical. Meanwhile, tensions continue to surge. US President Donald Trump has responded bluntly, saying the loss of the aircraft will not affect diplomacy. His words were direct. This is war. Trump has also declined to reveal how the US would respond if the missing crew member is captured or harmed. That silence is raising new questions about what comes next. A major development is now emerging from the Iran war, marking a significant escalation in direct confrontation between the United States and Iran. According to American media, it is now confirmed that a US fighter jet was shot down over Iran on Friday. US special forces have located one of the crew members. Multiple reports quoting sources say the aircraft involved was an F-15E Strike Eagle, a twin seat fighter jet used for both airtoair and airto ground missions. The jet was carrying two crew members, a pilot and a weapons systems officer. Reportedly, both crew members successfully ejected after the aircraft was hit by Iranian fire. While one person was rescued alive from inside Iranian territory, search operations are still ongoing for the second crew member. In a significant operational decision, Israel reportedly cancelled planned strikes inside Iran to avoid interfering with the ongoing rescue mission. Iranian state media was the first to report that the US jet had been shot down, though it claimed the aircraft was F-35. State television in Iran also broadcast a message saying that anyone who locates the US personnel would be rewarded by the government. This marks the first confirmed instance since the start of the war that a US fighter jet has been downed by Iran's fire. The White House has acknowledged the development. Press Secretary Caroline Levit told Axios that the president has been briefed on the situation. The F-15E Strike Eagle manufactured by Boeing is a key component of US air power. It is designed to carry out deep strike missions as well as engage in aerial combat. This incident comes amid mounting losses in the conflict. Earlier in the war, three F-15E aircraft were reportedly lost due to friendly fire. The human cost is also rising. At least 13 American personnel have been killed since the start of the campaign. This comes after Donald Trump and his defense secretary Pete Hegseath have both repeatedly suggested that the US has effectively won this war. Hegsathth and military commanders have spoken of air superiority and how degraded Iran's air defenses are. This shows that Iran still retains capacity to defend its skies. As the search continues for the missing crew member and tensions remain at a peak, this latest incident underscores the growing risks facing US forces in an increasingly dangerous and expanding conflict. Iran downs US fighter jet captures its pilot amid war. US military's frantic search for captured pilot fails. Choppers deployed to trace missing F-35 fighter pilot. After Iran shot down a US F-35 fighter jet on Friday, a video circulated on social media showing American helicopters and planes searching for the missing pilot in southern Iran's Kistan. Iranian media also urged residents to capture the pilot dead or alive for a promising reward. Given that the American F-35 fighter J of the Zionist regime, the provincial police force issued an urgent statement announcing that an American Zionist enemy F-35 fighter jet had been targeted and subsequently shot down within the nation's airspace in the central region of the country. And given the potential for the pilot or pilots of this fighter jet to land in Kyiluier and Buoyer Ahmmed province, we earnestly request all members of the general public, including our esteemed nomadic communities and villagers to extend their full cooperation to the military and law enforcement agencies. To the esteemed residents of Kiluya and Buer Ahmad province, you, our beloved, honorable and fiercely patriotic people, I urge you, should you manage to apprehend any enemy pilot or pilots and successfully deliver them alive and unharmed into the custody of our military and law enforcement agencies, you will receive a valuable reward and a bonus for good news. Iranian IRGC linked Tasnim news agency claimed that Iranian forces foiled the US military's bid to rescue the downed fighter jet pilot. Iran's farce news agency shared video showing a purported missile attack on an F-15 air base in Israel's Hifa. The Iranian air defense systems also claimed to have successfully downed an American UH60 Blackhawk helicopter in Iraqi airspace, but the reports remain unverified. Later, Iran's Tasnim news agency claimed that the US military was trying to extract the fighter jet pilot whose condition remained unclear. The operation involved Blackhawk helicopters and a C130 Hercules aircraft. However, their efforts have so far been futile, the agency said. Washington has not commented on any of the claims regarding the alleged downing of a jet or the rescue mission. In March, an American F-35 had to execute an emergency landing after being struck by Iranian fire. Iran has been carrying out retaliatory strikes on US and Israeli military installations in the Middle East. Meanwhile, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Friday vowed to crush Iran. Together with our American friends, we are continuing to dismantle the terror regime in Iran. We are eliminating commanders. We are bombing uh bridges and we are striking their infrastructure. In recent days, our air force has successfully destroyed 70% of Iran's steel production capacity. This is a truly significant achievement that effectively deprivives the revolutionary guards of both crucial financial resources and their capability to manufacture uh numerous weapons. In in full coordination between me and President Trump, between the Israel Defense Forces and the United States Army, we will continue to crush Iran. This regime is weaker than ever. Israel is stronger than ever. Iran has claimed a big strike on United States air force capabilities, having downed nine fighter jets. Iran cripples Trump's air power. Fighter jets downed. F-15, three in Kuwait, one in Thran. F-16, one in central region, one in south, F18, one in south, F-35, one in south, one in central region. The US Central Command in a statement rejected IRGC's claims saying, and I quote, "All US fighter aircraft are accounted for. Iran's IRGC has made the same false claim at least half a dozen times." The IRGC's claim comes amid Secretary of War asking the Army's top uniformed officer, General Randy George, to step down as the United States wages a war against Iran. George has been asked to take early retirement from the post of Army Chief of Staff which he has held since August 2023. The development comes as a new report claimed that Trump administration officials were hiding the data related to US troops wounded or killed in the Middle East.
